methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.environment_encryption
documentation:
The AWS KMS key that's used to encrypt your function's environment variables.

:default: - AWS Lambda creates and uses an AWS managed customer master key (CMK).

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.ephemeral_storage_size
documentation:
The size of the function’s /tmp directory in MiB.

:default: 512 MiB

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.events
documentation:
Event sources for this function.

You can also add event sources using `addEventSource`.

```
default

No event sources.
```

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.filesystem
documentation:
The filesystem configuration for the lambda function.

:default: - will not mount any filesystem

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.initial_policy
documentation:
Initial policy statements to add to the created Lambda Role.

You can call `addToRolePolicy` to the created lambda to add statements post creation.

```
default

No policy statements are added to the created Lambda role.
```

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.insights_version
documentation:
Specify the version of CloudWatch Lambda insights to use for monitoring.

:default: - No Lambda Insights

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.ipv6_allowed_for_dual_stack
documentation:
Allows outbound IPv6 traffic on VPC functions that are connected to dual-stack subnets.

Only used if 'vpc' is supplied.

:default: false

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.logging_format
documentation:
Sets the loggingFormat for the function.

:default: LoggingFormat.TEXT

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.log_group
documentation:
The log group the function sends logs to.

By default, Lambda functions send logs to an automatically created default log group named /aws/lambda/.
However you cannot change the properties of this auto-created log group using the AWS CDK, e.g. you cannot set a different log retention.

Use the `logGroup` property to create a fully customizable LogGroup ahead of time, and instruct the Lambda function to send logs to it.

Providing a user-controlled log group was rolled out to commercial regions on 2023-11-16.
If you are deploying to another type of region, please check regional availability first.

```
default

/aws/lambda/${this.functionName} - default log group created by Lambda
```

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.log_retention
documentation:
The number of days log events are kept in CloudWatch Logs.

When updating
this property, unsetting it doesn't remove the log retention policy. To
remove the retention policy, set the value to `INFINITE`.

This is a legacy API and we strongly recommend you move away from it if you can.
Instead create a fully customizable log group with `logs.LogGroup` and use the `logGroup` property
to instruct the Lambda function to send logs to it.
Migrating from `logRetention` to `logGroup` will cause the name of the log group to change.
Users and code and referencing the name verbatim will have to adjust.

In AWS CDK code, you can access the log group name directly from the LogGroup construct:

```
import * as logs from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-logs';

declare const myLogGroup: logs.LogGroup;
myLogGroup.logGroupName;
```

```
default

logs.RetentionDays.INFINITE
```

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.log_retention_retry_options
documentation:
When log retention is specified, a custom resource attempts to create the CloudWatch log group.

These options control the retry policy when interacting with CloudWatch APIs.

This is a legacy API and we strongly recommend you migrate to `logGroup` if you can.
`logGroup` allows you to create a fully customizable log group and instruct the Lambda function to send logs to it.

```
default

Default AWS SDK retry options.
```

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.log_retention_role
documentation:
The IAM role for the Lambda function associated with the custom resource that sets the retention policy.

This is a legacy API and we strongly recommend you migrate to `logGroup` if you can.
`logGroup` allows you to create a fully customizable log group and instruct the Lambda function to send logs to it.

```
default

A new role is created.
```

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.max_event_age
documentation:
The maximum age of a request that Lambda sends to a function for processing.

Minimum: 60 seconds
Maximum: 6 hours

:default: Duration.hours(6)

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.memory_size
documentation:
The amount of memory, in MB, that is allocated to your Lambda function.

Lambda uses this value to proportionally allocate the amount of CPU
power. For more information, see Resource Model in the AWS Lambda
Developer Guide.

:default: 128

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.on_failure
documentation:
The destination for failed invocations.

:default: - no destination

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.on_success
documentation:
The destination for successful invocations.

:default: - no destination

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.params_and_secrets
documentation:
Specify the configuration of Parameters and Secrets Extension.

:default: - No Parameters and Secrets Extension

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.profiling
documentation:
Enable profiling.

:default: - No profiling.

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.profiling_group
documentation:
Profiling Group.

```
default

A new profiling group will be created if profiling is set.
```

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.recursive_loop
documentation:
Sets the Recursive Loop Protection for Lambda Function.

It lets Lambda detect and terminate unintended recursive loops.

:default: RecursiveLoop.Terminate

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.reserved_concurrent_executions
documentation:
The maximum of concurrent executions you want to reserve for the function.

:default: - No specific limit - account limit.

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.retry_attempts
documentation:
The maximum number of times to retry when the function returns an error.

Minimum: 0
Maximum: 2

:default: 2

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.runtime_management_mode
documentation:
Sets the runtime management configuration for a function's version.

:default: Auto

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.snap_start
documentation:
Enable SnapStart for Lambda Function.

SnapStart is currently supported only for Java 11, 17 runtime

:default: - No snapstart

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.system_log_level_v2
documentation:
Sets the system log level for the function.

:default: SystemLogLevel.INFO

methods.name: cdklabs.generative_ai_cdk_constructs.DockerLambdaCustomProps.timeout
documentation:
The function execution time (in seconds) after which Lambda terminates the function.

Because the execution time affects cost, set this value
based on the function's expected execution time.

:default: Duration.seconds(3)
